Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client for whom construction projects are undertaken.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

name.description: The full name of the client organization or individual.

contactPerson.description: The primary contact person for this client.

contactEmail.description: The email address of the primary contact person.

contactPhone.description: The phone number of the primary contact person.

address.description: The physical address of the client.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a construction project associated with a client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

name.description: The name or title of the construction project.

description.description: A detailed description of the project's scope and objectives.

startDate.description: The planned or actual start date of the project.

endDate.description: The planned or actual end date of the project.

status.description: The current status of the project (e.g., 'active', 'completed', 'on-hold').

Instruction.title: Instruction
Instruction.description: Records a specific instruction given by a client on a construction site.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Instruction entity.

rawText.description: The original, unedited text of the client instruction.

recordedAt.description: The date and time when the instruction was recorded.

location.description: Specific location on the construction site where the instruction was given.

isUrgent.description: Indicates whether this instruction is marked as urgent.

recordedByUserId.description: The ID of the user who recorded this instruction, assuming an external user authentication system.

InstructionSummary.title: InstructionSummary
InstructionSummary.description: Stores the AI-generated summary of a client instruction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InstructionSummary entity.

summaryText.description: The concise, AI-generated summary of the instruction.

generatedAt.description: The date and time when the summary was generated.

ActionItem.title: ActionItem
ActionItem.description: Represents an actionable task extracted from a client instruction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ActionItem entity.

description.description: A clear description of the actionable task.

assignedTo.description: The individual or role assigned to complete this action item.

dueDate.description: The date by which the action item is expected to be completed.

status.description: The current status of the action item (e.g., 'pending', 'in progress', 'completed', 'blocked').

extractedAt.description: The date and time when the action item was extracted.

definition.description: Top-level collection for client entities. Each document includes `ownerId` (UID of creator) and a `members` map for client-specific authorization, facilitating authorization independence.

Top-level collection for construction projects. Documents include `clientId`, and denormalize `clientOwnerId` and `clientMembers` map (from the associated Client) to achieve authorization independence and support QAPs.

Top-level collection for client instructions. Documents include `clientId`, `projectId`, and denormalize `clientOwnerId` and `clientMembers` map (from the associated Client) for authorization independence and QAPs.
Subcollection for AI-generated summaries of instructions. Documents denormalize `instructionId`, `clientId`, `projectId`, `clientOwnerId`, and `clientMembers` for authorization independence.

params.name: instructionId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent instruction.

params.name: summaryId
params.description: Unique identifier for the instruction summary.

definition.description: Subcollection for action items extracted from instructions. Documents denormalize `instructionId`, `clientId`, `projectId`, `clientOwnerId`, and `clientMembers` for authorization independence.

params.name: instructionId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent instruction.

params.name: actionItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the action item.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and enables **Query-Assisted Path security (QAPs)** by heavily employing denormalization of authorization context. This design minimizes the use of `get()` operations in security rules, making them simpler, more robust, and easier to debug, while supporting atomic writes.

1.  **Clients (`/clients/{clientId}`):** This top-level collection stores client information. Each client document will include an `ownerId` (the UID of the user who created it) and a `members` map (e.g., `{ 'uid1': 'admin', 'uid2': 'viewer' }`). This membership map directly dictates who has access to the client and all its associated projects, instructions, summaries, and action items. This ensures direct, in-document authorization for clients.

2.  **Projects (`/projects/{projectId}`):** Projects are stored in a top-level collection. To maintain **Authorization Independence** from the parent client, each project document will **denormalize** `clientId`, `clientOwnerId`, and the `clientMembers` map from its corresponding client. This means that a project document contains all the necessary authorization information within itself, allowing security rules to evaluate access without needing to `get()` the parent client document. This also directly supports **QAPs** for projects, as rules can check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.clientMembers` for any `list` or `get` operation on `/projects`, enabling secure client-filtered queries without additional server-side logic.

3.  **Instructions (`/instructions/{instructionId}`):** Similar to projects, instructions are stored in a top-level collection. Each instruction document will **denormalize** `clientId`, `projectId`, `clientOwnerId`, and the `clientMembers` map. This ensures that authorization for any instruction can be determined solely by inspecting the instruction document, without requiring a `get()` call to its parent project or client. This again directly supports **QAPs** for instructions, allowing users to efficiently query all instructions belonging to clients or projects they have access to.

4.  **Instruction Summaries (`/instructions/{instructionId}/summaries/{summaryId}`):** Instruction summaries are stored as a subcollection of their parent instruction. To maintain **Authorization Independence**, each summary document will **denormalize** `instructionId`, `clientId`, `projectId`, `clientOwnerId`, and the `clientMembers` map. This ensures that access to a summary can be determined independently from its parent instruction or any higher-level entity.

5.  **Action Items (`/instructions/{instructionId}/actionItems/{actionItemId}`):** Action items are also stored as a subcollection of their parent instruction. Similar to summaries, each action item document will **denormalize** `instructionId`, `clientId`, `projectId`, `clientOwnerId`, and the `clientMembers` map. This allows for straightforward authorization rules that do not rely on `get()` operations.

This architecture ensures that security rules are simple, looking only at `request.auth.uid` and the `resource.data` of the document being accessed, thereby eliminating complex and often problematic `get()` calls across document hierarchies. It also inherently supports `list` queries with security rules, as all required authorization fields are present in the queried documents, enabling robust and performant data retrieval patterns based on user access.
